library_preparation_preflight.summary: Meaningful redis4cats-effects coverage normally exercises RedisCommands through Cats Effect against a Redis-compatible server; the artifact only brings cats-effect-kernel transitively, so tests need a concrete Cats Effect runtime and a Redis/Valkey service.

deterministic_setup.reason: redis4cats-effects_3:1.7.2 exposes APIs parameterized by Cats Effect typeclasses, but the resolved artifact only brings cats-effect-kernel transitively; tests using the documented Redis[IO] pattern need cats.effect.IO and the Cats Effect runtime.
The upstream redis4cats v1.7.2 test setup starts a Redis-compatible Valkey single node; effectful RedisCommands coverage requires a live Redis protocol server.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use a concrete Cats Effect runtime such as cats.effect.IO and put a redis4cats Log instance in scope, for example dev.profunktor.redis4cats.effect.Log.Stdout.given or NoOp.given. For single-node command coverage, target redis://localhost:6379. If covering keyspace notification behavior, start the server with notify-keyspace-events enabled, e.g. --notify-keyspace-events KEA.
risks: Upstream also tests replica and cluster modes with additional services; avoid requiring those unless simple single-node coverage is insufficient.
Redis.Pool APIs reference optional org.typelevel:keypool_3; add it only if pooled APIs are intentionally tested.
Netty/Lettuce native-image behavior may still require verification by the normal Gradle and Native Image tasks.
